name: Jambalaya One-Pot Meal
recipeIngredient: 1 medium green bell pepper, seeded, cut into strips
8 thin slices chorizo or other spicy sausage
1 pound medium shrimp, shelled and deveined
lemon slices and minced parsley - for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet or paella pan, heat oil over medium heat. Add garlic, onion, celery, and green pepper and saute just until softened (about 3 minutes).
2. Add rice, tomatoes and their juice, the water, hot-pepper sauce, ham (if used), chorizo, thyme, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 15 minutes.
3. Quickly add shrimp, cover, and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until rice is tender and shrimp have turned pink.
4. Toss with a fork. Garnish with lemon slices and parsley.
Paella One-Pot Meal:
1. In a large skillet or paella pan, heat 2 tablespoons olive oil.
2. Add 2 cloves garlic, minced; 1/2 cup chopped onion; and 8 thin slices chorizos (or other spicy sausage), broken into pieces with casings removed; saute for 3 to 5 minutes.
3. Add 1 cup long-grain white rice; 2 cups chicken stock; 1 whole boned chicken breast, cut into strips or bite-sized pieces; 1/8 teaspoon hot pepper sauce (optional); 1 teaspoon dried thyme or oregano; 1 teaspoon salt. Season with freshly ground pepper to taste.
4. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 15 minutes. Stir occasionally.
5. Add frozen petite peas (or 1 can drained artichoke hearts) and 1 pound medium raw shrimp, shelled and deveined. Cover and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until rice is tender and shrimp have turned pink.
